insight: Mexico's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup shows that they were able to gain momentum in half of the breaks they had, with an average change of -0.050 minutes per minute, which suggests a slight decline in their pace. This decline was consistent with the instances where they were not pressing before the break, indicating that Mexico tends to lose a bit of steam when they're not aggressively pushing forward. The fact that they didn't have any instances where they were pressing before a break means we can't draw conclusions about how that strategy affects their momentum. Overall, the numbers suggest that hydration breaks may slightly hinder Mexico's momentum, but more data is needed to fully understand the impact of these breaks on their gameplay.
